behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names all required variables (authorCache, commitMatch, bumpedPackages, changelogPath, versionHeader), functions (resolveAuthorForCommit, appendAuthors), regex pattern [a-f0-9], comparison expression prevPkg.version !== currentPkg.version, header format #### ${pkg.name}, and message 'No changelog entries'.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction over-specifies implementation: exact variable names (authorCache, commitMatch, bumpedPackages, changelogPath, versionHeader), exact function signatures (resolveAuthorForCommit, appendAuthors), exact comparison expression `prevPkg.version !== currentPkg.version`, exact regex character class `[a-f0-9]`, and exact output strings — leaving little for the agent to reason about.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific identifiers like `authorCache`, `commitMatch`, `bumpedPackages`, `changelogPath`, `versionHeader`, `resolveAuthorForCommit`, `appendAuthors` — alternative correct implementations using different naming would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Most f2p tests are pure substring/regex checks against the script text (e.g., `assert 'authorCache' in content`); only node --check, eslint, prettier execute anything, and none execute the script's actual changelog-generation behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub script containing only the literal strings/identifiers (e.g., comments mentioning `authorCache`, `bumpedPackages`, `async function resolveAuthorForCommit() {}`, `#### ${pkg.name}`) would satisfy every f2p test without implementing functionality.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Each behavior listed in instruction (changelog reading, bumped packages, author attribution, package headers, empty message) has a corresponding test.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with clear FAIL-TO-PASS / PASS-TO-PASS section headers and descriptive docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task produces markdown release notes, not structured data.

no_typos.reason: Filenames, variable names, and commands appear consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The script file is the obvious target; no specific line numbers leaked beyond what's needed.
